events.description: Join us on Thursday, May 28, from 5 to 7 p.m. for the community exhibition opening of Bison-tennial: From Colonization to Rematriation . This is a FREE community event. Bison-tennial explores the profound influence of bison on the history, cultures, and landscapes of the North American West. The exhibition traces their story from pre-colonial times through westward expansion and the near-destruction of the great herds—arriving at today’s growing restoration and rematriation efforts. The anticipated projects will include weed removal along the trail\(s\) corridor, hand-building additional beginner features on Lower Sparky’s, trimming back encroaching vegetation, and hand-rebuilding berms on Upper Sparky’s. events.title: (Re)sounding: Art Exhibition
events.description: Sound is not simply something we hear, but something we inhabit, shape, and are shaped by. Through immersive audio installations, evocative visual translations of sound, and interactive artworks that invite participation, the artists in (Re)sounding explore sound as a dynamic subject and material. This exhibition is an invitation to listen more deeply—to feel the weight and resonance of sound, to dwell inside it, and to hear the world in new ways.

Queen Nation consists of: Gregory Finsley on vocals & keyboards as Freddie Mercury, Mike McManus on guitar as Brian May, Pete Burke on drums as Roger Taylor, and Parker Combs on bass as John Deacon. The mission of the show is to carry on the musical torch and pay homage to the golden age of vintage Queen concerts. Queen Nation’s live 90-minute production of Queen’s greatest hits preserves the image, sound, and stage persona of vintage Queen. Audiences are amazed at the accuracy of Queen Nation’s live retrospective journey through such Qu...

events.title: Kenneth Peloke: Year of the Horse
events.description: Working in oil on large-format canvases, Kenneth Peloke approaches the horse not as subject but as a vessel for energy, instinct, and the kind of presence that resists easy translation into paint. His process is physical and deliberate, built through layers of mark-making that accumulate into something that feels less rendered than conjured. Year of the Horse brings together new works that explore what the animal has always represented in the human imagination: freedom, strength, the untamed thing we are drawn to and can never fully possess. events.title: Michael Kessler: All Resin
events.description: Michael Kessler has spent decades exploring the boundary between gesture and geometry, and his resin works bring that inquiry into sharper focus than ever. Each painting is built from up to fifty micro-thin translucent layers, fused and accumulated until something luminous emerges. Biomorphic forms weave through geometric structures, nature's logic made visible through an almost meditative process of application and restraint. Kessler likens his gestural freedom to a kind of painterly tai chi, controlled, but never rigid. In his all-resin works, that layered depth takes on new dimension. ...

events.title: Matt Flint: Sleeping in the Forest
events.description: Matt Flint doesn't begin with a plan. No sketches, no predetermined composition, just canvas, paint, and a process as intuitive as the landscapes that shaped him. Raised in rural Missouri and now living in Wyoming's Wind River Mountains, Flint has spent a lifetime in close study of the natural world, its textures, its rhythms, its constant state of becoming. That attention finds its way into every surface he paints. His works build through layers of creation and destruction, each image emerging as another recedes, holding the tension between what was and what might come next. The result i...

events.title: Tibet: The Roof of the World
events.description: 5:00pm on Wednesday, 17 June 2026 Community Room @ 1255 Park Avenue Americans can only visit Tibet as part of a pre-booked guided tour, but you can learn about this fascinating country in East Asia at the Park City Library. Experienced lecturer and new Parkite Deborah Jennings will give a lecture and a slideshow, then will take questions from the audience. She'll touch on geography, politics, religion, and c ulture - including yaks and butter, Buddhism and the Dalai Lama, and Mt. Everest. events.title: Teton House Concert: The Harp’s Voice
events.description: This event is sold out. To join the waitlist, please call the Box Office at 307.733.1128. Join Atlanta Symphony Principal Harpist Elisabeth Remy Johnson for an evening of transcendent beauty. Renowned for her tireless advocacy for women composers, Johnson brings the harp’s delicate resonance into the warmth of a home environment. This unique opportunity to witness the greatness of a true orchestral legend firsthand is not to be missed. events.title: Mozart, Prokofiev, & More
events.description: Mozart's gripping Overture to Don Giovanni opens the concert with ominous chords and cleverly placed silence, building tension before racing forward with exhilarating momentum. Prokofiev&\#03's sparkling Sinfonietta follows, bursting with playful rhythms colored by the composer&\#03's youthful wit and charm. The program concludes with Louise Farrenc's Symphony No. 3, an expressive work admired in her lifetime by musicians including Schumann and Berlioz for its elegant structure and dramatic range, moving between brooding drama and romantic warmth.

events.title: Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7
events.description: Promising a night of lyrical beauty and orchestral drama, conductor Louis Lohraseb shapes a program that follows an arc of adoration to heartbreak in the first half. The season finale at St. Mary’s Catholic Church opens with Wagner’s tender Siegfried Idyll, a deeply personal expression of love written as a birthday gift for his wife. Then the spotlight turns to Erica Petrocelli in two dramatic works that showcase the brilliance and expressive power of the soprano voice: Mozart’s virtuosic “Come scoglio” from Così fan tutte, a declaration of unwavering fidelity, and Mendelsohn's “Infelice,” ...

events.description: Elkhart Lake Fire Department’s Annual 4th of July Parade is on Sunday, July 5th, 2026 at 11:30am . There will also be a special appearance by the Wisconsin Badger Band! Afterwards head over to Fireman’s Park for food, drinks, live music and a 5th Quarter Show by the Wisconsin Badger Band. Parade participants are not required to pre-register for the parade. events.description: Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. On July 11 and 12, Chef Sarah Glover brings her signature, land-driven approach to the flame, preparing locally sourced game, fresh-baked breads, and seasonal ingredients over open fire. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer's showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan's plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Max Pachter and Evan deNicola of the "Eastern Dreamers" bring 70s Folk and Americana to life with their unique guitar-based approach to their performances and songwriting. Max's raw and smoky voice paired with Evan's pure and heady tenor create a contrasting, but soothing dynamic when harmonizing together. events.title: The Great Weed Roundup
events.description: Summit Land Conservancy and Summit County are looking for motivated volunteers to help us uproot noxious weeds! With spring fully in swing, join us for our next Stewardship Saturday at Miss Billie’s Preserve (Summit Community Gardens)! We will be pulling invasive dyer's woad, thistle, and white top while they are in bloom and before they can release their seeds. These are all aggressive, introduced plants that grow rapidly and create dense thickets which crowd out our native grasses and wildflowers. When we remove these weeds, we help to restore our local grassland ecosystem and ensure th...

Step into the ultimate zombie-infested race experience where every turn hides a threat, every shadow creeps with danger, and every step could be your last. The Freaky 5K isn’t just a run— it’s a fully immersive zombie apocalypse adventure built to test your speed, your nerve, and your will to survive. As you race through our terror-charged course, you’ll encounter: Chasing Zombies ready to hunt you down Action Obstacles designed to challenge your agility and k...
events.location: 2002 Olympic Dr, Midway, UT

events.title: The SteelDrivers
events.description: Grammy Award Winning Bluegrass band from Nashville. The SteelDrivers bring a passion to their delivery that adds a distinctive flavor, & forceful grit adding undertones of blues, soul, & rock.

events.title: Fables, Feathers & Fur
events.description: Fun for ages 3-6 Storytelling and art-making at the museum is an exciting opportunity for young visitors to engage with the art through looking, reading, and art-making in the galleries. All materials are provided. Walk-ins welcome! Registration is only required for groups of 10 or more. We can also accommodate educators interested in scheduling Fables, Feathers & Fur programming for their respective groups. For more information and to schedule your group, please contact swilson@wildlifeart.org . Generously sponsored by Nancy & Dick Collister.
events.location: Jackson, WY

events.description: Elkhart Lake’s Farmers & Artisans Market in the Village Square is a Saturday morning tradition. Pick up fresh seasonal vegetables, flowers, cheeses, local arts and specialty products from approximately 50 vendors. Every item sold is produced locally in the state of Wisconsin by the person right behind the stand! Grab your reusable grocery bags and head to the square every Saturday morning.

events.title: Old World Christmas Market
events.description: Reminiscent of the centuries-old Christkindlesmarkt of Nüremberg, Germany, the 27th annual Old World Christmas Market at The Osthoff Resort offers the warmth and merriment of this Old World tradition. For ten consecutive days, guests stroll the aisles of a grand heated tent bedecked with fragrant boughs of fresh evergreens and the tantalizing scents of roasted almonds and Nüremberg bratwurst as they browse for unique and specialty items among international and regional artisans. Czech blown-glass ornaments, Russian nesting dolls, German hand-carved smokers and nutcrackers, fine apparel, han...
events.location: The Osthoff Resort & Spa, 101 Osthoff Avenue, Elkhart Lake, WI
